comment: Operator-mandated 2026-05-10: assets in this list are NOT orphans -- they are finished, smoke-tested code that was built for a real use case but never wired into a live caller. They sit ready for hookup. Re-classify them as READY_FOR_HOOKUP in the inventory views, NOT ORPHAN. Renderer reads this file and overrides the analyzer's status when present.

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: Operator-facing voice-call alert wrapper. Sister to text_operator.py. Built iter 141.1 to provide an alert path that survives 10DLC/TFV regulatory failure modes -- voice goes via FCC rules, not TCR/A2P, so it works regardless of SMS campaign status. Single-line call site: `await voice_operator("RED throttle")` -> places voice call to OWNER_SMS_NUMBER. Strategic value: this is the alert path that stays alive when SMS provider is down or the campaign is rejected.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Need to identify the alert dispatcher that should call this when severity warrants voice over SMS. Likely candidate: throttle_system RED-state handler, sla_monitor critical-breach handler, kill_switch trigger.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Small (~30 min) -- add `from voice_operator import voice_operator` + call it in the existing alert dispatchers when severity == CRITICAL

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: Operator-facing SMS alert wrapper. NOT for driver/carrier comms (that lives in notification_service.py and is gated on A2P 10DLC APPROVED). text_operator targets OWNER_SMS_NUMBER with a single-line call site for internal alerts: throttle trips, SLA breaches, auto-bid wins, FMCSA-gate signals. Routes via TWILIO_MESSAGING_SERVICE_SID with TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER fallback.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Same as voice_operator -- needs to be wired into the alert dispatchers. Currently the alert system probably uses notification_service.py which is gated on A2P approval and could fail silently.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Small (~30 min) -- pair with voice_operator hookup; severity == HIGH triggers text, severity == CRITICAL triggers voice

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: FMCSA SMS BASIC Safety Score per-carrier scraper (iter 142.1 nationwide-vetted-carrier-DB Phase 2). Pulls Behavior Analysis & Safety Improvement Categories percentile scores: UnsafeDriving, CrashIndicator, HOSCompliance, VehicleMaint, ControlledSubstances, HMCompliance, DriverFitness. Each carrier's enriched sub-document upserts to db.carriers.sms.* keyed on dot_number. Real fraud-prevention layer: a carrier with brand-new authority + bad SMS scores is the classic shell-company pattern. CLI batch mode + per-carrier on-demand mode.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Need to register POST /api/carriers/{dot}/sms/refresh endpoint in server.py + extend backend/vetting_checks.py to call refresh_sms_batch() during carrier-intake. Also need a monthly cron for the bulk SMS CSV download path.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Medium (~2-3 hours) -- wire the endpoint, integrate the BASIC scores into the run_full_vetting() function in vetting_checks.py, add a cron for batch refresh

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: Vertical timeline component showing a driver's full notification lifecycle for ONE load -- dispatch sent/skipped/blocked + engagement events (link opened, voice answered, tracking confirmed). Color-coded dots (green/yellow/red). 45-second auto-refresh. Iter 100. Useful for: per-load dispatch diagnostic view, carrier-relations support workflow, troubleshooting why a driver didn't pick up.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Just needs to be composed into the load detail page or carrier-hub page. No backend changes -- it polls existing /api/notification-events endpoints.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Small (~15 min) -- add `<DeliveryReceipt phone={load.assigned_driver_phone} />` to LoadDetail or CarrierHub

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: UI badge showing whether the platform is running in shadow mode (running operations but not actually firing real outbound). Visible safety indicator while in pre-launch testing or during incident-response shutdowns. Color + label change with mode state.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Needs the shadow-mode flag concept defined in the platform. If the operator has decided shadow-mode isn't a thing they want to track visibly, this can be deleted instead.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: TBD -- depends on whether operator wants shadow-mode visibility. If yes: ~1h to define the flag in business_settings + render the pill in the top bar. If no: delete.

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: 3-layer parsing stack shipped 2026-05-10. Layer A (JSON normalizer) maps official-API responses for 5 boards (DAT/Truckstop/123/Direct Freight/SAM.gov) to ObservedLoad. Layer B (BS4 deterministic) parses HTML via selector-map dispatch + generic-heuristic fallback (already extracts 25 loads from real FreightFinder HTML without per-source selectors). Layer C (Claude haiku-4.5 LLM fallback) parses arbitrary HTML when Layer B returns []. Spend-capped at $5/day with race-safe atomic counter. End-to-end smoke-tested at $0.087/3 fixtures.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Awaits real load-board connection. Activation steps when load board connects: (1) fill SELECTOR_MAPS in html_parser.py:95-128 per source from observed HTML, (2) register parser_failure as new source in self_healing/anomaly_dispatcher.py (~30 lines), (3) flip CHL_SELF_HEAL_PHASE{1,2,3}_ENABLED feature flags, (4) wire scraper output to db.market_loads_observed via existing board_adapter_normalizer.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Small (~1-2 hours per board for selector maps, ~30 min for self-heal source registration). Architecture is in place; the work is per-source calibration not new code.
ready_for_hookup.current_state: LIVE in code (3-layer dispatch endpoint /api/scrapers/parse-test reachable + auth-gated). Dormant in workflow (no scraper cron firing, no parser_failures collection populating). Calibration phase begins on first real load-board payload.

ready_for_hookup.intended_use: 18 load board adapter classes scaffolded in loadboards.py (DAT, Truckstop, 123Loadboard, Direct Freight, SAM.gov, Loadsmart, UberFreight, TruckSmarter, ChlDirect, FMCSA Census + 8 niche: BulkLoads, LivestockTalk, LivestockTransportNation, HopLoads, FR8Star, TankTransport, AgriCarriers, OnlineFreight). 5 have JSON normalizers in board_adapter_normalizer.py; 13 lack normalizers + many lack credentials.
ready_for_hookup.hookup_blockers: Per-board: (a) operator obtains API credentials or confirms public-HTML-scraping is acceptable per ToS; (b) write the normalizer function mapping that board's response shape to ObservedLoad; (c) register in BOARD_NORMALIZERS dict; (d) populate public_rss_sources.json with feed URL or HTML endpoint. Gated downstream on FMCSA broker authority (~May 13) for credentialed boards.
ready_for_hookup.estimated_hookup_effort: Medium (~1-2 hours per board for normalizer + smoke test against real response sample). Total backlog: ~13-26 hours across all 13 unfilled adapters. Niche boards (8) likely HTML-only, will use Layer B once selector maps are written.
